name: Cart Abandon / Step-1-Not-Step-2 Recovery
summary: Recovers buyers who entered their contact info on Step 1 of a 2-step order form (the 'Brunson Box') but never completed payment on Step 2. The 2-step form exists specifically so the email is captured as a micro-commitment BEFORE billing, which makes this drop-off recoverable at all. Maps to the Cookbook 'Drop-Off Automation' Message #1/#2/#3 for the 'step-1-not-step-2' leak.

trigger.primary_event: Step 1 of the 2-step order form submitted (name + email + address captured) AND no completed order within the delay window
entry_conditions: Contact submitted the address/contact step of a 2-step order form (tag e.g. `order-step1-started`)
No `order-completed` / purchase event fired for that contact within the wait window
exit_conditions: Purchase completes at any point (remove from sequence immediately to avoid post-purchase asks)

sequence.beat: The helpful nudge — 'did something go wrong?' Removes friction, assumes a technical hiccup, not a no.
sequence.delay: 15-30 minutes after Step 1 with no completed order
sequence.subject_angle: Curiosity + service: "Did your order go through?" / "Quick question about your order…"
sequence.body_framework: Jim Edwards 'If you want X, do this' (warm-traffic formula). Open friendly, name the exact product they were buying, give the ONE clear instruction to finish (single CTA link straight back to Step 2 with their info pre-filled). Make it about THEM, single action only.
sequence.copy_notes: No discount yet. Pure friction-removal. Honest, no fake scarcity.

sequence.beat: Agitate the gap + reinforce desire — remind them WHY they wanted it; handle the silent objection.
sequence.subject_angle: Problem/desire: "Still thinking about [result]?" / "The [product] is still in your cart"
sequence.body_framework: Jim Edwards Problem / Agitate / Solve. Restate the problem the product solves, agitate the cost of staying stuck (loss aversion — 'people move away from pain more than toward pleasure'), then re-present the offer as the solution. One or two proof points (testimonial / guarantee). Single CTA back to Step 2.
sequence.copy_notes: Ten Reasons People Buy: lead with escape-pain + save-time. Add the guarantee here to neutralize risk.

sequence.beat: Honest urgency / last call — real reason to act now, then a stealth soft-close.
sequence.subject_angle: Urgency: "Last call on your [product]" / "Closing your order out today"
sequence.body_framework: Jim Edwards honest-urgency close + Stealth/Columbo 'By the way…' P.S. State the REAL reason the window closes (cart/price/bonus expiring, limited stock) — must be true and honored. Recap the offer in one line, single CTA, then a P.S. soft-close.
sequence.copy_notes: Never invent scarcity you won't honor (Edwards rule + Soap Opera 'fake urgency backfires'). If genuinely evergreen, use a 24h bonus/coupon that truly expires.

copy_persona.primary: jim-edwards-copywriting-secrets (Copywriting Secrets) — The Direct-Response Copy Closer
scripts_used: 3 Sales Formulas: 'If you want X, do this' (warm) for Msg #1; Problem/Agitate/Solve for Msg #2
Honest scarcity/urgency rule for Msg #3 (honor every deadline)
Stealth / Columbo soft-close ('By the way…') in the P.S.
Ten Reasons People Buy — tie escape-pain + save-time + save-money
Hot/Warm/Cold routing: treat abandoners as WARM (they already raised their hand) — lead with the solution, not the problem
copy_persona.structural_persona: russell-brunson-the-funnel-hackers-cookbook — supplies the 2-step order form ('Brunson Box') structure and the Drop-Off Automation Message #1/#2/#3 map that makes this leak recoverable
copy_persona.rationale: The Cookbook is explicit: a 2-step order form 'captures the micro-commitment and enables step-2 follow-up' — wrong order form for the price (one-page form on a low-ticket offer) makes this recovery impossible. Edwards writes the sentence-level copy.

ghl_build.engine: Skill 44 (caf / convert-and-flow) — builds the GoHighLevel Automations-area workflow from an ordered build-plan JSON (every action carries order/parentKey before first save; builder fails loud on any non-2xx).

trigger_config.ghl_trigger: Order Form Submission on Step 1 (or 'Abandoned Checkout' if the funnel emits it) — fired when contact info is captured but order not completed
trigger_config.alternative: Tag Added = `order-step1-started` (set by the Step-1 form) used as entry; filter: contact does NOT have tag `order-completed`

actions_in_order: 2. If/Else — condition: contact has tag `order-completed`? TRUE -> end workflow (Goal: purchase). FALSE -> continue
4. Send SMS — Message #1 mirror (optional, short link to Step 2)
6. If/Else — purchased? TRUE -> end. FALSE -> continue
9. If/Else — purchased? TRUE -> end. FALSE -> continue
10. Send Email — Message #3 (honest urgency + Stealth close)
ghl_build.goal_event: Purchase / `order-completed` tag — wired as a workflow GOAL so the contact exits the instant they buy (prevents emailing a customer asking them to buy what they already bought).
if_else_branches: After every wait: re-check `order-completed` -> exit on purchase
Optional: branch SMS vs email-only on contact DND / phone-present
ghl_build.notes: GHL Automations have no public API — caf builds the workflow shape (trigger + waits + if/else + email/SMS steps); the operator pastes finalized email/SMS bodies. Honor any countdown timer used on the order page so the urgency in Msg #3 stays true.

books: The Funnel Hacker's Cookbook (2-step order form / Brunson Box; Drop-Off Automation Message #1/#2/#3; 'wrong order form for the price')
DotCom Secrets (2-Step Tripwire / Free-Plus-Shipping order form; Follow-Up Funnel)
Copywriting Secrets — Jim Edwards (PAS, 'If you want X', honest urgency, Stealth close, Ten Reasons People Buy)
source.fidelity_note: Built strictly from the extracted Cookbook drop-off map ('step-1-not-step-2 -> Message #1/#2/#3') and Edwards' documented email formulas. No invented mechanics.

flexibility.principle: This template is a recommended default and a resource — it is NEVER a rule or a requirement.
flexibility.mode_1_user_has_explicit_desire: Do exactly what the user wants. This template is an optional reference only; it is never imposed or overridden onto their choice.
flexibility.mode_2_user_is_unsure: Suggest this proven template and explain why it works, but let the user decide.
flexibility.mode_3_user_wants_it_handled: Build the whole automation from this template.
flexibility.note: Always overridable, mixable, customizable, or ignorable. It assists; it does not dominate.
